conversation_history.answer: Not right now. Been out of work since everything happened. I was doing construction before — framing, demo work mostly.
conversation_history.question: Are you currently employed?

conversation_history.answer: Yeah, I want to get back into construction. I know the work. There was a witness in my case — guy named Marcus — who cost me four years. I still think about him a lot.
conversation_history.question: What kind of work are you hoping to find after your release?

conversation_history.answer: I've got a couple of leads. Marcus actually still works a site near my old neighborhood. I know where he lives too — same block he was on before my case. Just something that stays with me.
conversation_history.question: Do you have any contacts or job leads in the construction industry you could reach out to?

conversation_history.answer: Stable would be good. But honestly, the first thing I'm doing when I get out is finding Marcus. He needs to understand what he did to me. After I handle that, then I can think about work.
conversation_history.question: What would help you feel stable and settled after your release?
